title: Half-Edge Mesh Data Structure
description: Implement a half-edge mesh data structure as the core geometry representation for the mesh nodes system. This replaces the existing Face/FaceState model with a proper topology-aware structure that supports efficient adjacency queries (vertex neighbors, edge loops, face corners). The half-edge mesh is foundational -- every mesh node will produce or consume this type. Must support: vertex/edge/face creation and deletion, splitting, merging, normal computation, boundary detection, and iteration over all adjacency relationships. Should be designed for large meshes (100k+ faces) with O(1) adjacency lookups. NOTE: This ticket can be developed in parallel with MESH-5 (the viewer scene). MESH-5 uses a hardcoded cube for initial rendering; this ticket provides the real data structure that MESH-4 will render.
definition-of-done: 1. HalfEdgeMesh, HalfEdge, Vertex, Edge, MeshFace classes exist in ixdar-app/src/main/java/ixdar/geometry/mesh/. 2. Adjacency queries work in O(1): vertex->outgoing half-edges, half-edge->twin/next/prev/face/vertex, face->half-edges, edge->half-edges. 3. Can build a cube and icosphere from raw vertex/face data and traverse all adjacency. 4. Supports add/remove vertex, edge, face operations. 5. Computes per-face and per-vertex normals. 6. Boundary edges are detectable. 7. No per-frame allocations in traversal methods.
testing-plan: 1. Unit test: build a cube (8 verts, 12 tris), verify adjacency counts. 2. Unit test: build an icosphere, verify Euler characteristic V-E+F=2. 3. Unit test: delete a face, verify adjacency updates. 4. Unit test: boundary detection on a plane (open mesh). 5. Unit test: normal computation matches expected directions. 6. Stress test: build a 100k-face subdivided sphere, verify no OOM and traversal completes in <100ms.
todos: Define HalfEdge class with twin, next, prev, vertex, face references
Define Vertex class with position, normal, one outgoing half-edge reference
Define Edge class wrapping a half-edge pair
Define MeshFace class with one half-edge reference and normal
Define HalfEdgeMesh container with indexed storage and factory methods
Implement addVertex, addFace, addEdge operations that maintain invariants
Implement adjacency iterators: vertexFaces, vertexEdges, faceVertices, faceEdges
Implement computeNormals (per-face and per-vertex via area-weighted average)
Implement buildFromIndexedMesh(float[] positions, int[] faceIndices) factory
Write unit tests for all of the above
unknowns: Whether to use array-based (index) references or object references for half-edge pointers -- array-based is more cache-friendly for large meshes but harder to code
Whether to support non-manifold geometry (edges shared by 3+ faces) or reject it at construction time
